reasoning:
We are 38 days from the December 31, 2025 resolution date. South Park has been incessantly provoking Trump since its 27th season premiere in July 2025, heavily mocking his litigiousness, his $16M settlement with Paramount, and even portraying him in explicitly sexual situations (with Satan, and most recently with JD Vance in a November 13, 2025 episode). Despite these extreme provocations, and despite the White House openly criticizing the show as 'fourth-rate', Trump has not initiated any legal action or issued a public cease and desist specifically against South Park. 

Legal experts uniformly note that parody is strongly protected under the First Amendment (Hustler v. Falwell), which makes winning a lawsuit virtually impossible, likely deterring Trump's legal team. Furthermore, the resolution criteria require that any legal action must 'result in negative repercussions' (penalties, settlements, restrictions, loss of revenue). Given South Park's ironclad contracts and massive $1.5B deal, forcing restrictions or a quick settlement within the next 38 days is highly improbable, even if a lawsuit were filed today. Since no legal action has manifested since July—or in the 10 days since the newest controversial episode—the probability is steadily decaying. I estimate an 11% chance that action is taken and causes negative repercussions before year-end.
